title: Evangelion Manga Screenshot Thread
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Manga Screenshot Thread             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Jan 02, 2015 5:56 am I'm reading the Evangelion manga and it is wonderful. One of the main advantages of the comic is that it has a much better idea of how to tell a joke than the series. This gag, for example, is a variation on one from the series, but it has much better delivery. Kaji doesn't imply that he slept with Misato, but rather simply reveal that he knows how she sleeps, which is a believable slip of the tongue. Also, Rei is given a hilariously nonchalant reaction. Say, let's have an Evangelion manga screenshot thread. That's a good excuse for me to share the above image, as well as this letter to the editor about why anime/manga seems better than American animation/comics.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jan 02, 2015 8:39 am Huh? Rei is present at that point in the manga? She wasn't in the show so is there a reason why in the manga?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jan 02, 2015 9:08 am Um, because the manga is different? There are many much, much bigger differences (think Kaworu, and the ending).

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jan 02, 2015 9:25 am xPearse wrote: Huh? Rei is present at that point in the manga? She wasn't in the show so is there a reason why in the manga?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jan 02, 2015 9:33 am I do like Sadamoto’s “Why tell the same story twice?” approach to Eva. There’s be no point to reading it if it were only Sadamoto refusing to screen-cap the show for panel art while he quotes everything line-for-line, scene-for-scene. I also like Sadamoto’s humor in the manga. I think it easily helps it stand apart from Anno’s approach to the series.

[]: Re: Evangelion Manga Screenshot Thread             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 02, 2015 9:44 am Tumbling Down wrote: I'm reading the Evangelion manga and it is wonderful. One of the main advantages of the comic is that it has a much better idea of how to tell a joke than the series. This gag, for example, is a variation one one from the series, but it has much better delivery. Kaji doesn't imply that he slept with Misato, but rather simply reveal that he knows how she sleeps, which is a believable slip of the tongue. Also, Rei is given a hilariously nonchalant reaction.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Fri Jan 02, 2015 10:05 am ...on a side note, I think it's a brilliant visual gag of the three girls with their jaws dropped open, but one for entirely unconnected reasons (that IS a lot of food on that spoon). Granted, he's not so much of a 'in your face card' as he was in the show, but something about this Kaji (the way he drops the line) still has that implication of intimacy with Misato that she (and Asuka, for separate reasons) would find shocking and embarrassing, both for revealing Misato's sleeping habits in public (which is always embarrassing even if most of the people present know), and opening the door as to WHY he is familiar with those sleeping habits. And the innocuousness of it is ballsy on it's own: 'What? I'm not saying ANYTHING like that. What would make you imply such a thing?' So, the anime one was more crass (which was amusing on it's own), but the manga one is subtle.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jan 02, 2015 10:18 am @ Monk Ed: You aren't familiar with the brand of humor used in the military aren't you? And Kaji spent the past weeks surrounded by military people.

[]: Re: Evangelion Manga Screenshot Thread             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Jan 02, 2015 10:37 am Monk Ed wrote: Rei's non-reaction is indeed hilarious but I still remember how I felt when the same joke happened in the anime and I liked it better because it had so many layers of meaning (and wrongness!) to it and did a lot to characterize Kaji. The way the joke is presented here seems like it would be out of character for his anime characterization; he's not some bumbling unintentional implier of unfortunate things, he is these-are-my-balls-look-at-my-balls-my-balls-are-amazing ballsy about everything he does. What made it so shocking in the anime is that he was very clearly intentionally joking that Shinji was fucking Misato, and that's just wrong on so many levels and the fact that he gets away with it in how nobody in the audience thinks of him as a creep forever after that communicates a really fundamental aspect of his character, especially metatextually: Kaji is able to get away with so much shit that would get any man in real life discarded by society as the lowest of the low, and nobody in the audience even bats an eye at it. That is the greatest joke of all.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Jan 02, 2015 12:25 pm Oh boy, did I find some foreshadowing?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jan 02, 2015 1:44 pm ^ Mythology Gag rather, since in Sadamanga there isn't any fapping in the hospital.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jan 02, 2015 7:49 pm ElMariachi wrote: They aren't in the Over the Rainbow in that scene, in the manga Shinji and co doesn't visit Asuka in the fleet and the Gaghiel fight happens off-screen (and Asuka solo him!), the pic posted here happen in NERV's cafeteria, explaining Rei's presence.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Fri Jan 02, 2015 9:55 pm It's funny to because Asuka wants Shinji to do that to, too bad it's near the end she realizes he won't because Shinji is well Shinji. Later she teases him by making him think he was about to see her naked body because she only had a towel on, but then she had her clothing under it.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jan 03, 2015 12:16 am ElMariachi wrote: ^ Mythology Gag rather, since in Sadamanga there isn't any fapping in the hospital.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Jan 03, 2015 12:36 am Tumbling Down wrote: I thought I might like this manga better than the anime, but that's a damn big thing to cut out. Is there at least some sort of equivalent plot point? Y'know, Shinji doing something horrible just for the sake of doing something horrible and then despairing over it?

[]:             Quote Post by Cosmo11 » Sat Jan 03, 2015 12:43 am The manga hospital scene doesn't have Shinji doing that. But it's still creepy I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sat Jan 03, 2015 12:58 am Someone got their comeuppance for EoE. Avoided some issues too no doubt.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Jan 03, 2015 1:10 am AR-99 wrote: Someone got their comeuppance for EoE. Avoided some issues too no doubt.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 03, 2015 3:35 am Never thought of it that way, but interesting! That whole scene has nice parallels with some other things in the manga as well as NGE anime, but I won't say anymore for folks who are just reading it first time through.
